{{Quote|Do you not realize how many Sith are here in Dreshdae?|[[Lashowe]]|<ref name="KotOR" />}}
'''Dreshdae''' was a settlement on the planet [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]] that existed before the [[Jedi Civil War]].

==History==
{{Quote|This is Sith space now, and the company has to survive regardless of who is in charge. The Republic, the Sith, eh... the economy has to keep going. |[[Unidentified Czerka representative|A Czerka representative]]|<ref name="KotOR" />}}
After the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], it became somewhat of a capital for the [[Planet/Legends|planet]], providing a tavern called [[The Drunk Side]] with criminal activities, trade, transport docking, [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] runs, erotic entertainment, and hangouts for [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]], [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]], assassins, and [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] trainees from the [[Sith Academy (Korriban)|Sith Academy]] nearby.

[[Duron Qel-Droma]], [[Shaela Nuur]], and [[Guun Han Saresh]] investigated Korriban for signs of a [[Terentatek/Legends|terentatek]] during the [[Great Hunt (Jedi)|Great Hunt]], gathering information at the settlement. After Guun Han left to find another one on [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]], which led to his death, Duron and Shaela searched the [[Valley of the Dark Lords/Legends|Valley of the Dark Lords]] for the monster.

[[File:Dreshdae2.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The Dreshdae settlement circa [[3993 BBY]]]]

Duron died at the hands of the beast and received one last vision of the future, where a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] named [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] would take the Qel-Droma robes, kill the creature, and defeat [[Darth Malak]]. His vision came true decades later as Revan and the ''[[Ebon Hawk]]'' crew sought the [[Star Map]]s that led to the [[Star Forge]].

After the [[Jedi Civil War]], fragmentation and unrest rioted throughout the Sith Academy, with rival groups seeking to hold power; by the end of the infighting, the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] ranks were devastated, and the academy was abandoned, taking a large portion of Dreshdae's clientele and users. According to [[Kreia]], Korriban was abandoned when the Republic explored it following the end of the [[Jedi Civil War]]. The Republic eventually established a presence on the world after the [[Dark Wars]] and watched over it until the arrival of the resurgent [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|Sith Empire]] from [[Dromund Kaas/Legends|Dromund Kaas.]] 

Dreshdae survived the Jedi Civil War and was the only inhabited [[City/Legends|city]] on Korriban when the [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]] reestablished the Korriban Sith Academy during the [[New Sith Wars]], in [[1006 BBY]].

[[23 BBY/Legends|One year before]] the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]],<ref name="The Essential Reader's Companion">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> a group of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] led by [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Siri Tachi/Legends|Siri Tachi]] [[Mission to Korriban (23 BBY)|visited]] Dreshdae while looking for [[Granta Omega]] and [[Jenna Zan Arbor]]. In addition, the [[Commerce Guild/Legends|Commerce Guild]] built several offices there.<ref name="Jedi Quest: The Final Showdown">''[[Jedi Quest: The Final Showdown]]''</ref>

Approximately one year after the Clone Wars, the scientist [[Jenna Zan Arbor]] was spotted by [[Ferus Olin]] in the city, while he and [[Trever Flume]] were waiting for their transport to be refueled. Her business on the planet went unexplained.<ref name="Ghosts of the Sith">{{InsiderCite|88|Ghosts of the Sith}}</ref>

Dreshdae, along with the abandoned settlement of [[Kaniset]], were known to be among the ruined city of [[Vardin]]'s rivals in size at the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Behind the scenes==
In the game ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'', Dreshdae was misspelled as "Dreshae."
